title: hoheria lyallii
intro: Hoheria lyallii, also known as the Lyall's bellflower or mountain lyallia, is a flowering plant native to New Zealand. It has been used in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Hoheria lyallii include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of the plant are often dried and used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
